description: This figure presents engineering stress-strain curves of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel after different warm multiaxial forging (MAF) passes, covering four samples: MAF-0 (0 forging passes, solution-treated initial sample), MAF-1 (1 MAF pass), MAF-3 (3 MAF passes), and MAF-5 (5 MAF passes). The x-axis records engineering strain, while the y-axis denotes engineering stress in MPa. A clear trend is observed: yield strength and ultimate tensile strength rise significantly with increasing MAF passes, while fracture elongation (ductility) decreases. Specifically, MAF-0 delivers a yield strength of ~380 MPa, ultimate tensile strength of ~762 MPa, and elongation of ~75%, while MAF-5 achieves a yield strength of ~1528 MPa, ultimate tensile strength of ~1548 MPa, with only ~8% elongation. Work hardening capacity drops sharply with more MAF passes, ascribed to increased dislocation density and grain refinement from MAF processing. This figure validates that warm MAF effectively strengthens the studied low-density steel via combined dislocation strengthening and grain boundary strengthening.
